title: Distributed Card Testing: High Global Velocity, Normal Per-Merchant Activity
narrative: Compromised card credentials are being distributed across multiple fraud operators or automated testing scripts, each using the card at different merchants at a controlled pace. While activity at any single merchant (MID) appears normal with reasonable transaction frequency and amounts, the card-level counters reveal the card is cycling through many distinct merchants simultaneously. The fraudster network maintains per-merchant discipline to avoid velocity flags at individual acceptance points, but the aggregate card footprint exposes coordinated unauthorized use. This pattern mimics a cardholder making legitimate purchases across various retailers but the counter aggregations reveal an implausible diversity of simultaneous merchant relationships and product categories that exceed normal multi-channel shopping behavior.
indicators: C1 > 60 (p90), indicating elevated rolling card activity counter
C8 > 29 (p90), high card-to-merchant diversity in recent window
C14 > 7 (p90), card used across many distinct merchant categories
card1_distinct_addr1 > 8, card associated with numerous shipping addresses
C11 / C8 < 1.5, ratio indicating single or few transactions per merchant
TransactionAmt between 60-180 (p25-p75 range), amounts within normal cardholder pattern
D1 > 100 OR D1 = 0, device-card pairing either completely new or suspiciously reused
indicator_explanations: Card shows significantly elevated total transaction activity in the monitoring window
Card has been used at an unusually high number of different merchants recently
Card transactions span far more merchant category codes than typical cardholder behavior
Card is being shipped to many different delivery addresses in short timeframe
Each merchant sees only one or two transactions, avoiding per-MID velocity triggers
Individual transaction amounts look normal and fit cardholder spending habits
Device relationship is either first-time pairing or implausibly high reuse count
summary: Card exhibits normal per-merchant transaction behavior and reasonable amounts, but card-level counters reveal simultaneous use across an implausible number of merchants, categories, and shipping addresses. Each merchant sees controlled activity while the aggregate footprint exposes distributed unauthorized use.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders rarely transact across 30+ merchants in a short window while maintaining single-transaction relationships with each. This controlled-per-merchant, high-aggregate-diversity pattern indicates stolen credentials being systematically tested or exploited across a distributed fraud operation designed to evade velocity controls at individual acceptance points.
